name: Batter-Fried Squash Blossoms from Loren Marti
recipeIngredient: 3 dozen squash blossoms (male, just about to open)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a shaker jar, combine milk, flour, salt, and pepper.
2. Place squash blossoms in a large pie tin and gently pour the milk-flour mixture over them.
3. Heat the oil in a large heavy skillet until a drop of water sizzles when placed in it.
4. Fry the batter-coated blossoms in the hot oil until golden brown; drain on paper towels.
5. Sprinkle with paprika and serve hot.
Squash blossoms are considered the greatest of delicacies by the Zuni. The choicest of all are the largest male flowers, which are carefully gathered from the vine, fried in deep fat, and served as an appetizer or used as a seasoning for vegetables, soups, and stews.
